title: ABC9: Cell Port Bug Patch
body:
Fixes a bug that causes `2.44.16. Executing ABC9_OPS pass` to crash. 

Within ABC9: if a cell's ports weren't fully specified (e.g. 8 bit port, but only 4 bits were set) and those ports had a setup time requirement (aka arrival time) then an exception would occur when trying to set those arrival times.

I added a test file which exhibits this error, but it is not minimal. 

My run looks like this, with `synth_xilinx` being the relevant part of course: 
```
read_verilog /path/to/design/des50.v
synth_xilinx -dff -flatten -noiopad -abc9 -edif des50.edif
write_verilog -noexpr -norename /path/to/output/des50_syn.v
```

Follow up: 
There's also the question about why only 4 bits of the 8 bit port were set, as this concerns the RAMB36E1's write-enable port -- this should be populated by Yosys since it is doing RAM inference. 
--> Fixing this will also make the test case obsolete for the bug above, but I'm not sure how to address this.
